sourceMovie.title: Son of a Gun
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Son of a Gun (2014) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Julius Avery's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Street Kings
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Son of a Gun clicked for you, Street Kings (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Undisputed II: Last Man Standing
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Son of a Gun clicked for you, Undisputed II: Last Man Standing (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Son of a Gun?
faq.answer: Try Street Kings, Undisputed II: Last Man Standing, Heist—each shares DNA with Son of a Gun while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Son of a Gun?
faq.answer: Son of a Gun (2014) is best described as Action, Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Son of a Gun worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
